svg.text: 05 - API, WebSocket, and Event Contracts
What this covers: externally visible interfaces + internal event model for real-time behavior.
API Foundation
Base: `/api/v1` REST + `/api/v1/ws/chat/:conversationId` WebSocket. Auth: Bearer JWT except auth endpoints.
Error envelope standardized with code/message/details. Rate limits and pagination are first-class API behavior.
REST Resource Surface
Auth: magic-link, verify, refresh, logout.
User: me read/update profile and preferences.
Trips: create/list/get/update/delete + activate + complete.
Itinerary: generate/get/update + day-level read/update + swap apply.
Conditions: current, forecast, activity assessment endpoint.
Briefings: list/today/day/regenerate.
Swaps: list/get/accept/decline.
Conversations: list/create/messages history.
Attractions: list/search/details/tips.
Operational API Rules
- Auth limits stricter than general API; regeneration and chat message limits explicitly bounded.
- Cursor pagination for list-heavy datasets (attractions/conversations/history).
- 422 validation paths are expected and should return actionable schema errors.
- Idempotency considerations for trip activation/swap acceptance endpoints.
WebSocket Protocol (Chat Streaming)
Connection lifecycle
connect -> authenticate -> bi-directional messages -> heartbeat every 30s -> idle timeout around 5m.
auto-reconnect with exponential backoff + last message id for dedupe/session continuity.
Client message types
`message` for free text, `selection` for structured choice payloads.
Server stream types
`stream_start` -> multiple `stream_token` -> `stream_end` with full content and structured_data artifacts.
Structured payloads drive UI components (choice cards, summaries, actions).
Concurrency and Safety
Single active WS per conversation per user to avoid state divergence.
Backpressure strategy required for slow clients during token bursts.
Graceful close semantics to avoid orphaned generation tasks.
Background Tasks + Internal Events
Celery queues partitioned by workload: briefings, monitoring, notifications.
Schedules include hourly condition refresh, periodic schedule checks, and alert checks.
Key tasks: generate morning briefing, refresh conditions, detect significant changes, dispatch push payloads.
Event Catalog (MVP in-process bus)
trip.created, trip.activated, trip.completed, itinerary.generated, itinerary.updated,
swap.suggested, swap.accepted, swap.declined, conditions.changed, briefing.generated, notification.sent.
Freshness SLAs (system-level)
Weather current: refresh hourly-ish, short cache. Forecast: multi-hour refresh windows. Tides/solar: daily-ish with longer TTLs.
Trip/itinerary/profile are authoritative DB state (no staleness tolerated on writes).
Implementation Non-Negotiables
- contract tests for REST + WS payloads
- typed schemas shared across service and client boundaries
- replay-safe task design and idempotent state transitions
- observability: queue lag, WS disconnect rates, token latency, briefing generation failure rates
Next recommended read: 06 - Data Model and Storage Schema.
